notes:
Ember 1.9.2 — Stable PIN fix, two-finger scroll, stronger ad blocking

• Ember Link's PIN could occasionally regenerate itself after a close+reopen (a partial write to its save file could look 'corrupt' on the next launch) — the PIN is now written atomically so it can never be silently swapped out from under a paired phone.
• Two-finger scroll on a trackpad/mouse now works over the phone remote's touchpad and live screen mirror (it only sent clicks/drags before — a physical scroll gesture did nothing).
• Ember Browser's ad/tracker blocking now shares Ember's system-wide Ad Blocker list instead of a separate, much smaller hardcoded one — turning on a bigger list (or adding a custom domain) there now also strengthens in-browser blocking.
• Fixed a silent-failure bug in the Features directory: a broken feature used to just close the dialog and do nothing, with no error shown — failures now surface properly.

Builds on 1.9.1's Kindle Ember Link fixes and 1.9.0's Ember Link connect-from-anywhere.
